summary: Node.js driver fails to recover from unresponsive Primary
description:
If the Primary server stops responding over the TCP connection, but the connection itself is not terminated (which can happen in case of network problems), then the driver gets stuck and can not failover to the other members of the replica set. The problem is easily reproducible by running replica set locally and issuing a SIGSTOP to the primary server.

I have created a simple test application (with setup scripts included), that can be used to illustrate the problem:
[https://github.com/OleksandrChekhovskyi/mongo-replset-test]
Exact repro steps are described in the README file.

It seems that the problem is that ismaster ping is done sequentially for all servers, so if the first one gets stuck, it's never going to get the new configuration, because it keeps disconnecting/reconnecting.

summary: ignoreUndefined in find
description:
Hi,

I've just spend an hour trying to debug a {{find}} query which has an undefined value.
First problem is that logging the undefined value with JSON.stringify will not display anything (is there a debug util for this?). After I found out, I checked why our global {{ignoreUndefined = true}} was not working for the query.

I found https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-563 which shows why the default ignore undefined mode was reverted to serialize to null again. I think this is exactly the right thing for the query part of updates, and to keep things consistent, find should behave the same way...
But, there are no comments indicating this decision in https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/2.2/lib/collection.js#L179 which is unfortunate for a user that's debugging the code.
It, imho, would be helpful to say something about these decisions in a comment in the code.

To summarize:
- comment in Collection.find why {{ignoreUndefined}} is not copied from {{this.s.options}}
- a util function that can debug a js object that will be used as bson.

summary: Driver does not exit gracefully while in reconnecting state
description:
REPRO:
Call MongoClient.connect and obtain a Db object (testing with a single Server)
Bring down mongod while the app is running.
call db.close(true, callback) and wait for callback to fire.
Ensure that no other ref'd handles are keeping node alive yet it does not exit

THOUGHTS:
I did a bit of debugging and it's being kept alive by two timers (both within mongodb-core):

1) one is issued by checkStatus() within Pool.destroy. I think this one is functioning correctly because it thinks there's something important in the work queue and it's going to admirably die trying to flush that deferred op before exiting. The question is why is something in there even though I queued no operations, and I've determined that it's an item queued by monitoringProcess in Server. Ironically it's not passing options.monitoring=true to flag the operation as not important. I've verified that disabling monitoring in the options works around this issue. This timer will otherwise keep the app active indefinitely. 

2) Pool.destroy does not call clearTimer() on self.reconnectId if it's non-null and it probably should. All that happens is we're forcing the app to stay alive until that times out and then no-ops since the state is destroying/destroyed, so seems safe to early cancel it. This only delays app shutdown by up to the retry interval, but since this is often longer than shutdown grace periods given by things like docker the effect is the same we get a "dirty" shutdown.

foo.movie

{noformat}

summary: Possible memory leak in Replset Topology Monitor
description:
We've been debugging memory leak issues in our node application using LLDB and noticed a large number of Timeout classes sitting in memory. Digging into it,w e found there were over 1000 timeouts (which had already executed) attached to the ReplSet, as you can see in the output below (from just a few hours of running).

0x00003b5ae4974ec1:<Object: ReplSet properties {
    .domain=0x0000084bfe804201:<null>,
    ._events=0x00003b5ae4985041:<Object: EventHandlers>,
    ._eventsCount=<Smi: 19>,
    ._maxListeners=0x0000084bfe804311:<undefined>,
    .id=<Smi: 1>,
    .s=0x00003b5ae4985059:<Object: Object>,
    .authProviders=0x00003b5ae49796b1:<Object: Object>,
    .initialConnectState=0x00003b5ae49850e9:<Object: Object>,
    .state=0x00002ba102135261:<String: "connected">,
    .haTimeoutId=0x0000084bfe804201:<null>,
    .authenticating=0x0000084bfe804421:<false>,
    .ismaster=0x00003b5ae4985119:<Object: Object>,
    .intervalIds=0x00003b5ae4985151:<Array: length=1083>}>

Looking at the code in mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset.js and the toggleMonitor function, you see timeouts being pushed onto the intervalIds array (which you can see has 1083 timeouts on it in the LLD output). However, I don't see anywhere that these timeouts are being removed from the array once they fire.

Use actual server type in standalone SDAM events:

Use actual server type in standalone SDAM events:
Currently the server type inside the topologyDescriptionChanged event
for a single topology is 'Standalone', which is unexpected. According to
the SDAM specification a TopologyDescription has a list of
ServerDescriptions, which had a type field indicating the type of server
it is and does not state that the server type never changes if the
topology is single.

My expectation is that a single connection to a replica set primary, for
example, would give me a topology type of 'Single' and a server type of
'RSPrimary', not 'Standalone' like it currently does.

The serverDescriptionChanged event properly has the type set.

I could not get the tests running locally - they just stop after the first step `[purge the directories]` - hence I have not added tests but the existing sdam server test should still pass.

Add .replaceRoot to AggregationCursor
description:
MongoDB 3.4+ supports a $replaceRoot stage while aggregating: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/operator/aggregation/replaceRoot/#pipe._S_replaceRoot

`lib/aggregation_cursor.js` seems to define prototype / class methods for method chaining instead of using an array/object-based raw pipeline. `.replaceRoot({ newRoot: '$xyz' })` isn't available there yet and I'd like to add it to make our code (which uses chaining elsewhere) consistent.

I would be happy to submit a pull request for this, the only thing I was unsure about was how to limit the command to servers of version 3.4+ only, and whether this is necessary. Can someone advise me on this?

summary: Have TCP keepalive default to true
updates scram.js hi() algorithm to utilize crypto.pbkdf2Sync()
description:
From github pull request https://github.com/christkv/mongodb-core/pull/184 .

This pull request fixes a few different issues.

The previous hi() algorithm was basically a manually written version of crypto.pbkdf2Sync(). My guess is that this library predates the addition of that method in Node v0.9.3. That method in crypto is a C++ native algorithm so it's able to perform the computations dramatically faster. In example on my machine, the previous hi() algorithm would take around 200-300ms while the same pbkdf2Sync version only takes around 12ms.

The hi() function is deterministic in the sense that for the same arguments you get the same result back, always. The reason this matter is that when using hi() each worker in the pool will call hi() with the exact same arguments. That means it's doing the same computationally intense call for each worker in the pool even though all of them will utilize the same resulting string. In this fork I'm storing the result of the previous call so that way it only performs the computation once per boot.

After the clientFinal generation it appears there was some left-behind code. // Generate server key and // Generate server signature. Neither of those calls appears to have their result used anywhere in the rest of the function. Removing them appears to have no regression.

If you have questions let me know. Thanks.

summary: Fix .cursor() method documentation in Core Server, Replset and Mongos
description:
The docs for mongodb-core for the classes {{Server}}, {{Replset}} and {{Mongos}} list the arguments for the {{.cursor()}} method as {{ns, cmd, callback}}. This is incorrect: the third argument is an {{options}} object.

This method returns a cursor. The docs currently do not say what this method returns.

Link to the relevant methods in the docs:
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/Server.html#cursor
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/ReplSet.html#cursor
* http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/core/api/Mongos.html#cursor

summary: Cannot projection field name is 'partial'
Create MongoNetworkError and update relevant error handlers
description: Create a new {{MongoNetworkError}} class specifically for network-related errors. {{MongoNetworkError}} will extend {{MongoError}}. Then go through update relevant error handlers of network-related errors to use {{MongoNetworkError}} instead of {{MongoError}}. This will facilitate determining whether an error is "resumable" for the purpose of Change Streams (NODE-1057).

summary: initializeUnorderedBulkOp should not reject Promise on writeErrors
description:
When doing a initializeUnorderedBulkOp, and (at least) one write operation fails, if I pass a callback (err, result), I get both the error and the result, where the error is pretty much useless, but in the result I can get the WriteErrors explaining what failed.

But when I don't pass a callback and get a promise back, the promise gets rejected, and all I get is a useless error in the .catch, but I have no way of getting the full WriteErrors.

I believe the Promise returned should be rejected only when the whole operation fails (eg. connection error) but if one of the many operations in the bulk fails, it should still resolve the promise, and the developer should manage exceptions.

summary: Metamocha does not support Mocha options
description: When [using Mocha programatically|https://github.com/mochajs/mocha/wiki/Using-mocha-programmatically], the docs specify that you can pass in a list of options when creating your Mocha instance. Currently, Metamocha does not allow for this, and we would like to add it in to make Metamocha as flexible as Mocha is.

summary: Metamocha doesn't filter tests correctly
description: Currently, Metamocha applies every filter to every test, and if the test passes one filter, then it's included in the run. Also, filters assume that every test has metadata relevant to that specific filter, and we would rather have filters skip tests that don't have relevant metadata

summary: Remove Change Stream Db support
description: Change Streams no longer support being created on databases. The currently-written code for this needs to be stripped out and eventually placed into a new PR.

summary: Fix .skip and .only in Metamocha
Switch mongodb-test-runner to not depend on npm's version of mongodb-core
description: We want the test runner to depend on the local version of {{mongodb-core}}, rather than the version available on npm. To do this, we need to recursively traverse up the dependency tree to find the version of {{mongodb-core}} that uses {{mongodb-test-runner}} as a dependency.

summary: the result of db.dropDatabase is "true" but the database and it's contents still exist when I check from the terminal.
add support for { nameOnly: true } to `listDatabases`
description: Right now the `listDatabases` command returns a list of the databases on a server. In Mongo 3.2.13 they added the capability to return only the database names, without the stats. This allows it to not need locks in order to return the data.

summary: updateOne and updateMany must contain at least one atomic operator
Aggregation commands are required to have a `cursor` key
description:
The CRUD spec specifies: 
{code}
/**
   * Indicates whether the command will request that the server provide results using a cursor.
   *
   * If true, this option is sent with a value of "cursor: {}". The default value is true.
   * For servers < 2.6, this option is ignored and not sent as aggregation cursors are not available.
   *
   * @see https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/aggregate/
   */
  useCursor: Optional<Boolean>;
{code}

The node driver does not follow this behavior, and it's become a problem because support was deprecated in version 3.4 of the server, and fully removed in 3.6.

This will have to be fixed for both the 2.x and 3.x branches of the node driver, with one behavioral change:
  - 2.x will continue to massage the resulting cursor into the expected array form, so as not to break existing applications
  - 3.x will simply always return an aggregation cursor

process crashes when certain connection string parameters are specified.
description:
```MongoClient.connect('mongodb://localhost/db?maxIdleTimeMS=100', (err) => {})``` will crash the running process.

"*maxIdleTimeMS*" is unsupported, but instead of just shutting down the process, it should probably emit a warning and dismiss the unsupported parameter, or try...catch and call the callback function with the error as first parameter.

The ```parse``` function throws  in ```url_parser.js:234```.
The caller is ```mongo_client:289```

So my suggestion is :
* Emit a warning and ignore the parameter
* try...catch the call to that function in mongo_client.

summary: No way to detect when replica set becomes unavailable
description:
We are developing a microservice which integrates with a MongoDB Server backend. We support the MongoDB Server in a standalone configuration. When the MongoDB Server becomes unavailable (e.g., when the {{close}} event on {{db}} is raised), we put the service into a paused state. When then attempt to reconnect (via {{MongoClient.connect()}}) in background to go from a paused to a running state.

We would like to support replica set configurations, as well, but note that the {{close}} event is not raised by {{db}} in this configuration. We need to be agnostic on the MongoDB Server configuration as it is provided by our customers. We are looking for a consistent way to detect when either a standalone server or an entire replica set is no longer available.

summary: Disambiguate native and core ReadPreferences
description:
There are currently two differing implementations of {{ReadPreference}} - one in core and one in native.  They do very similar things, but are not exactly the same which leads to a number of confusing call sites in the code, and further does not allow us do things like {code}if (options.readPreference instanceof ReadPreference){code}.  We should remove one of the implementations (in native), and only use the one from core throughout both codebases. Also, this is needed to be able to enforce read preferences for change streams.

Currently the {{ReadPreference}} defined in native is being exported, so this is going to potentially be a breaking change for users which needs very clear documentation.

summary: Clean up `Collection.prototype.find` parameter handling
description:
The `find` method on `Collection.prototype` has gone through many painful iterations which has left it in a troublesome state in terms of parameter management.  It would seem that the signature for this function should be something like `function(query, fields, options, callback)`, however it has to do a number of checks to verify that previously supported forms aren't being used.  Most importantly, it lacks almost any documentation because there was an emphasis on using chaining to support all the additional features of find.

I think we should break this in 3.x, and only support `find(query, options, callback)`.  `fields` actually just turns out to be an alias for `project`, and we already have a chainable helper for this.  This also allows us to remove a ton of type checking code in the method itself.

**NOTE:** This will almost certainly cause some users serious pain, but if we properly document the change and how to migrate away from it I am confident the end result works better for all parties.

summary: readPreference of Secondary can cause Node driver to hang
description:
When trying to utilize a readPreference of Secondary on a replica set, the Node driver has a chance of intermittently taking forever to run.

MongoDB: 3.4.6
Node: 6.10.3
MongoDB Node Driver: 2.2.30

I have attached my [^topology.js] that I used for testing as well as the logs from the runs to this ticket.

I set up a 3.4.6 PSS replica set on my local computer with the ports of 27017, 27018, and 27019. I used the instructions in the [Node Quick Start guide|https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/quick-start/quick-start/] to install the MongoDB Node Driver. In order to get increased logging, I utilized the ["simple code example" for Topology Monitoring|https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/reference/management/sdam-monitoring/] as the basis for my application. The only substantive changes were:

1. An adjusted connection string.
2. Timestamp logging to get an idea of how fast the application runs.
3. A {{findOne()}} command against a non-existent collection.

Between each test, the only thing I changed was the connection string in two different ways:

1. I changed the name of the hosts. They were either "localhost" or the FQDN of "Hatcher.local". Some runs had only the first hostname as the FQDN with the rest being localhost.
2. I changed the order of the hosts. The Primary was either the first node listed in the connection string or the last node.

|| Hostname || Order || Approx. Time || Logs ||
| localhost (all) | PSS | 13ms | [^localhost_Primary.log] |
| localhost (all) | SSP | 14ms | [^localhost_Secondary.log] |
| FQDN (first) | PSS | 10,016ms | [^hatcher_Primary.log] |
| FQDN (first) | SSP | 9ms | [^hatcher_Secondary.log] |
| FQDN (all) | PSS | canceled operation after 20 minutes | [^hatcherall_Primary.log] |
| FQDN (all) | SSP | 7ms | [^hatcherall_Secondary.log] |

summary: Change Streams update operations aren't triggered properly when resuming from previous change id.
description:
Consider the following scenario (based on sample code stored at https://github.com/rlondner/mongodb-node-changestreams-sample):

1. Run `node produce.js` to insert the first document into a `devices` collection
2. Run `node listen.js` to simulate an application listening to change streams from the `devices` collection
3. Run `node produce.js` to insert a second document into a `devices` collection. The listen.js process should pick up the change.
4. Run `node update.js` to update all the documents of the `devices` collection. The listen.js process should pick up the 2 changes.
5. Stop the listen.js process and start it again
6. Run `node produce.js` to insert a third document into a `devices` collection. The listen.js process should pick up the change.
7. Run `node update.js` to update the third document. The listen.js process +*doesn't pick up the change*+.
8. Now stop the listen.js process and delete the file in the localStorage folder (which stores the change stream the listen process should pick up from).
9. Start the listen.js process again
10. Run `node produce.js` to insert a fourth document into a `devices` collection. The listen.js process should pick up the change.
11. Run `node update.js` to update the fourth document. Note that the listen.js process *does* pick up the change.

I am using the `promoteBuffers: true` option on `MongoClient.connect()` in order to get back node.js `Buffer`s from the driver for normal collections that I use, which is great as it helps to avoid explicit conversions in my code.

However, this option is causing GridFS to fail with uncaught error attached below.

The issue seems pretty obvious - the data of the gridfs chunk is also being promoted to `Buffer` but then the code tries to call the function `doc.data.length()` which makes sense for `BinData`, but `length` is a simple number property for `Buffer`.

summary: move connection string parser to mongodb-core
description: Whether we can expose the connection string parser has been an oft debated point, and an ask from a number of parties. Since we now need this for the SDAM refactor, as well as the fact that the feature is a "core" concept of MongoDB drivers, I think it's time to expose it in our core driver.

summary: db.collection catching application errors and calling the callback twice
description:
db.getCollection is catching our application errors and calling the callback we pass to it twice.

We call db.collection has follows:

db.collection(collectionName, callback);

The callback then executes our application code (in the same tick). If we throw an error it is being caught by the code in db.collection which then calls the callback again which I believe is incorrect behaviour.

I can see this was attempted to be fixed 8 months ago by @christkv but it looks like the line that actually makes the fix work is commented out:
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blame/2.2/lib/db.js#L466

I've also noticed that a lot of the asynchronous methods in the mongo driver invoke handleCallback which introduces an asynchronous break to the code flow. Would that be an alternative fix for this issue?

https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/934a43a90141fbacebd4bcdd9151a4f6a8a8bc57/lib/mongo_client.js#L498

It also says startSession will return a Promise:
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/934a43a90141fbacebd4bcdd9151a4f6a8a8bc57/lib/mongo_client.js#L499

However startSession actually returns a ClientSession. I'm really confused how should I use startSession...

summary: Move responsibility for terminating sessions to topology, and track all sessions
description:
While investigating NODE-1326, I am finding a lot of issues related to how the SessionPool tracks sessions.

If we can assume that a topology only ever belongs to one MongoClient at a time, and that the topology gets created/destroyed alongside the MongoClient, then I think we should implement the following:

1. Track all active and available ServerSessions, and all active ClientSessions, from within the SessionPool. Currently, we track all active ClientSessions from the MongoClient, and all available ServerSessions inside the SessionPool.
2. Ensure that the topology is responsible for sending an endSession for all ServerSessions when the topology is closed.

[~matt.broadstone] [~jessica.lord]

summary: Driver 3.x is much slower against Atlas than 2.x
Decrease likelihood of implicit session leaks
description: Spec change [here|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/472d8f4486f9c020c4f9608a93a763c2845c4949]

summary: MongoDB 3.6 URI does not parse into component parts correctly when the auth database and database being connected to are different
description:
The latest driver seems to have a problem with the case whereby the auth database and the database being connected to are different, or at least it seems to be a problem from 3rd party systems such as Mongoose when using the 3.6 URI syntax.

The MongoDB Atlas, says that even on a v3.6 compliant driver I should use the _/mytestdb_ syntax to set my database name even though I authenticate against the admin database as shown in the example below:

*mongodb+srv://test-user:3#f!69A8AZu9@cluster0-abcde.mongodb.net/mytestdb*

In the case of Mongoose they use the MongoDB driver library url_parser to parse the URL and get the DB name. In the latest version of the driver this seems to return 'admin'.

If I use the 3.4 syntax as shown below it seems to work fine:

*mongodb://test-user:3#f!69A8AZu9@cluster0-shard-00-00-abcde.mongodb.net:27017,cluster0-shard-00-01-abcde.mongodb.net:27017,cluster0-shard-00-02-abcde.mongodb.net:27017/mytestdb?ssl=true&replicaSet=cluster0-shard-0&authSource=admin*

I am not sure if you would consider this a bug as it does not seem that the URL Parser is supposed to be a public API, but the url_parser does seem to have code that tries to set dbName to something other than its default of 'admin' in certain cases, and it works for the 3.4 syntax so I am assuming it is a bug.

summary: mongodb-extjson should allow non-strict (relaxed) parsing
ReadPreference should not be taken into consideration when the drop database command is invoked
Crush in mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset_state.js

I'm seeing a random crushes when running multiple heavy updateMany calls against replset.
{code}
/srv/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset_state.js:149
  var serverName = server.name.toLowerCase();
                         ^

TypeError: Cannot read property 'name' of null
    at ReplSetState.remove (/srv/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset_state.js:149:26)
    at Object.handler [as cb] (/srv/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/topologies/replset.js:1221:28)
    at Connection.<anonymous> (/srv/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:299:33)
    at Connection.g (events.js:291:16)
    at emitTwo (events.js:106:13)
    at Connection.emit (events.js:191:7)
    at Socket.<anonymous> (/srv/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/connection.js:256:10)
    at Socket.g (events.js:291:16)
    at emitNone (events.js:86:13)
    at Socket.emit (events.js:185:7)
{code}

Servers are running mongodb 3.2.8
Driver version: 3.0.5

summary: Driver Performance Benchmarking
description:
Implement the standard driver performance benchmark suite.

Spec here: https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/benchmarking/benchmarking.rst

summary: improve APM api
description: Our APM interface is awkward. You need to call a floating function {{instrument}} in the top level export, which patches the prototype of a number of different internal types, and then leaves you to create a {{MongoClient}} assuming everything worked. Other drivers allow you to pass in some sort of command listener object, additionally allowing you to determine _which_ commands you'd like to listen for. We should clean this up and provide our users with a better interface.

summary: refactor write concern decoration
description: We currently decorate commands with write concerns in a number of disparate places ([bulk|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/bulk/common.js#L19], [admin|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/admin.js#L168], [collection|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/collection.js#L2258], [collection again|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/collection.js#L2980], [db|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L477], [db again|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L1652], [gridStore|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/gridfs/grid_store.js#L1568]). These should be consolidated into a single path, and ideally placed in core, rather than native.

summary: Node Driver Hangs Indefinitely When Connecting Via SRV Record
honor user provided batchSize in aggregate
description: We currently ignore any provided batchSize, and default to {{batchSize: 1000}} for {{aggregate}}.

summary: Cursor session id (none) is not the same as the operation context's
Deprecate snapshot option
Document minSize option
description: As seen in NODE-1433, we do not document the {{minSize}} option. We should add it to our jsdoc.

summary: Use the connection string parser from mongodb-core in native
description: The v3.0.2 release of {{mongodb-core}} included an exported connection string parser. We should remove the old code in {{node-mongodb-native}} and instead use this parser.

summary: Extended json package does not decode canonical dates when relaxed is true
Non-write commands are sending txnNumber
description: We are sending {{txnNumber}} on find commands, which is causing an error on 4.0 feature sets.

summary: bson.Binary breaks in extended json stringify() when constructed with Uint8Array
description:
There's a few issues here:

The constructor for Binary seems to want to expect that it will only receive a few valid types (string, Array, Uint8Array, Number).
https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/ffed2c45377a253a054355f56e681867f58ca93e/lib/bson/binary.js#L42-L57
However this isn't the case, because any non-null object that isn't a string or number will just get assigned to the buffer, e.g.
{code}
> console.log(new bson.Binary(/foo/))
Binary {
  _bsontype: 'Binary',
  sub_type: 0,
  position: undefined,
  buffer: /foo/ }
{code}

Additionally, the extended json code assumes that an input Binary object will always contain a Buffer:
https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-extjson/blob/a911f8d22c33dc433995a1a25ffd69fc0053a022/lib/bson/binary.js#L13

This means that Binary objects that were constructed with any type other than a Buffer will generate invalid output from stringify().

To fix this, either the Binary type's constructor should be more strict about what types it accepts (or convert input types into Buffer) or the stringify() code should be capable of generating the valid base64 from a Array/Uint8Array, etc.

summary: Inconsistent results for BSON.Binary when used with mongodb-extjson on Web
findOneAndUpdate
description:
Hello,

 
The methode findOneAndUpdate with a empty _update field $unset all the field. :_
 
collection.findOneAndUpdate(\{ _id: new ObjectID(id) }, {}, \{ upsert: false })
 
_I know that if empty i should prefer use only the findOne method but it seems dangerous to unset all if empty in this method. and i need to use it like this. Expect to do nothing if empty._
 
 
_Dor now i make the trick to protect against the $unset all with :_ 
collection.findOneAndUpdate(\{ _id: new ObjectID(channelId) }, \{ $setOnInsert: { insert: true } }, \{ upsert: false })
 
Can you update this rules?

summary: Remove all node version filters less than 4.x
description: We see test filters like {{node: '>0.8.x}}. Since we only suport {{4.x}} or higher, we should remove these filters and/or update them.

summary: Remove all test error/log output from tests
description: When we run out tests, sometimes we have stray {{console.log}}s or uncaught errors that disrupt the output of the test runner. Let's clean that up.

summary: extjson: support encoding and decoding RegExp's
description: We've seen a few Stitch users trying to pass in {{/someregex/}} to Stitch which does not get encoded to a BSON regex. If there was a way to automatically encode these and separately a way to choose to decode these to RegExp (less important), that would be very nice.

summary: Nodejs API docs should have a title
description: [http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.0/api/] has "index" as title, which isn't helpful, above all when bookmarking this page

summary: mongoErrorContextSymbol undefined in isGetMoreError() in change_stream.js
description: In {{isGetMoreError()}}, we return {{!!error[mongoErrorContextSymbol].isGetMore;}}. This assumes that {{mongoErrorContextSymbol}} is always defined, which may not be true. We should check to make sure {{mongoErrorContextSymbol}} is defined before accessing {{isGetMore}}.

summary: Change Stream Resuming Broken for Promise-based call to .next
description: Change Stream's Resume functionality is broken for non-callback based calls to {{.next}}. The callback for {{cursor.close}} needs to be wrapped in a promise if no callback is present.

summary: tests encoding functions differs in output between versions of node
description: Our {{serializeFunction}} method depends on the output of {{Function.prototype.toString}}, which has changed in recent versions of node. As a result, a number of our bson tests are failing due to an expected output string of {{function () {}}} rather than {{function() {}}}.

summary: Wrong documentation for Cursor.addQueryModifier value parameter
description:
The documentation for [Cursor.addQueryModifier|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/Cursor.html#addQueryModifier] wrongly documents the *_value_* parameter as being of type _boolean_.
This seems to be a Copy/Paste oversight from the preceding [Cursor.addCursorFlag|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/Cursor.html#addCursorFlag] method doc.

summary: Cursor: When Cursor is exhaust hasNext() function returns error instead 'false' when it is called more than once.
Cursor.forEach should support returning a Promise
Resync retryable writes tests
description: Resync retryable writes spec tests.

summary: Incorrect example within Create a Text Index section of Create_Index tutorial
description: Within this example, based on the explanation, the code should be changed. The field should be called comments or content.

summary: Documentation for using Cursor.returnKey is incomplete, missing
description:
The official documentation for Cursor.returnKey does not explain what values can be passed to it.

The cursor example for every method in the class shows "10" as a valid value, but does not explain what it means:
{{collection.find({}).returnKey(10)                              // Set the cursor returnKey}}

The doc for the method itself ends abruptly before explaining what value to pass it:
{{/**
 * Set the cursor returnKey
 * @method
 * @param {object} returnKey Only return the index field or fields for the results of the query. If $returnKey is set to true and the query does not use an index to perform the read operation, the returned documents will not contain any fields. Use one of the following forms:
 * @return {Cursor}
 */}}

This is an issue with the documentation on the website as well, which matches the javascript documentation: http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/2.2/api/Cursor.html#returnKey

summary: Cursor count() ignores collation
cursor.stream should return a readable stream, and not mutate the cursor
description:
See hhttps://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/7a89d40b0d8a8475ac86ddc40957858d37d2f33b/lib/cursor.js#L1100

Mutations here actually break the changestream behavior. We should return a ReadableStream that subscribes to the cursor's output.

summary: Only send bypassDocumentValidation if it's true
description: See spec changes [here|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/pull/266/files].

summary: Unify read preference resolution
description: We have a few ways of working with read preferences: {{convertReadPreference}}, {{getReadPreference}}, {{other-hand-rolled-methods}}. Some of these methods do not even follow the well-defined inheritance rules set forth in the server selection spec. We should unify all of these methods into a single on, so we can begin to standardize only having to change a single function.

summary: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests
description: Re-sync transaction spec tests for new UnknownTransactionCommitResult label tests. See changes:  [https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/f76bc24087d914dd5f1257b16b817457a7fb4cfa]

summary: Improve specificity of write concern errors
description: The latest slew of retryable write updates improved the driver by properly reporting write concern errors as errors, client-side. This was a good start, but we can still improve on this design by creating a more specific error class (e.g. {{MongoWriteConcernError}}) so we can catch this specific error type for more complicated logic elsewhere.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description:
If drivers have already implemented DRIVERS-464, they need to update their implementation of how they initially store {{startAtOperationTime}}. Instead of setting a default {{startAtOperationTime}} when the ChangeStream is created, drivers must omit a default {{startAtOperationTime}}. When resuming, drivers must use the {{operationTime}} of the initial aggregate command as the default {{startAtOperationTime}} value.

See https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/commit/745debcf4624ec820eb9b138cd8f28fa7b1e5782 for the exact implementation details.

summary: Provide Transactions example for Docs
Change stream should pass both resumeAfter and startAtOperationTime
retryWrites does not seem to work if any update multi=true queries are executed
description:
retryWrites does not seem to work if any update command with multi=true are executed, but the only place to set retryWrites is on the connection string, and there seems to be no way to override or suppress this when updates to multiple documents are required.

I get this error:

MongoError: Cannot use (or request) retryable writes with multi=true

The retyWrites option should just be ignored where it is not supported, or there should be a way to suppress or override the setting with an options property

summary: All operations should be moved to `lib/operations/<class>_ops.js`
description: Presently {{operations}} live in the code right next to wherever they are being used (e.g. {{Collection.prototype.createIndex}} and {{createIndex}} in that same file). This not only leads to a decrease in readability of these (already large) files, but also an inability to share code in these operations between methods. The problem with not being able to share these is that we currently have operations that need to use other operations (e.g. https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/collection.js#L1387), and in these cases we now have a heavyweight chain of calls to `executeOperation` which incurs non-trivial overhead. Instead these operations should be moved to their own file(s), and allowed to be shared as "private implementation" of the driver.

summary: Kerberos semver should allow for other patch versions
description:
See https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/issues/127#issuecomment-397682616

This error is due to some of the intricacies of semver. Essentially, b/c we are pre-1.0, we *only* allow kerberos version {{0.0.23}}, even though we have version 0.0.24. We should probably fix the semver range to allow other patch versions of kerberos to be installed.

summary: Attach command response to MongoWriteConcernError
description:
In changing the pool's behavior to throw an error when it encounters a {{writeConcernError}}, we inadvertently broke some behavior for the bulk API that was already accommodating this case. We should modify our now-official {{MongoWriteConcernError}} to also include the full response, if {{ok: 1}}. 

Additionally, our changes have broken expected user api for {{bulk}}. In this case, we need to explicitly catch {{MongoWriteConcernError}}s, and wrap them to look like they once did. Once this is complete, the tests in {{replset_operations_tests.js}} will pass.


NOTE: remember to reenable tests in {{replset_operations_tests.js}} when this work is complete

summary: Cursor.prototype.count() is not respecting parent ReadPreference
Add `toBSON` method to `ClientSession`
description: When the driver erroneously attempts to serialize a {{ClientSession}} to the wire (often a result of some options error, or the like), we end up with a very unhelpful cyclic reference error. We should provide a {{toBSON}} method on {{ClientSession}} that throws a more useful error message.

summary: Remove JSDOC that says [param=null]
description: {{[param=null]}} specifically means a value of {{null}}, not that the parameter does not exist. AFAIK we do not actually rely on passing around the value {{null}}. In most of these cases, it is meant to say that the parameter may not exist, which is denoted in JSDOC with a simple {{[param]}}.

summary: Expose Kerberos warning message
description:
See https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/issues/127#issuecomment-397682616

We should probably have some sort of way to alert the user to this sort of situation.

summary: Missing shallow copy in DB.collection
description:
3.1 got released a couple of hours ago and took down my server. The issue looks to be a bug in 3.1 that is triggered by a bug in my code.

For some reason my code was doing:

const collection = this.db.collection(name, name);

i.e. passing a string to the options arg. This worked in 3.0 but breaks in 3.1. The reason is that in 3.1 the shallow copy is broken.

3.0: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0/lib/db.js#L412]
3.1: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/db.js#L316]
 
Not the object.assign isn't assigned to options? I presume it should read
options = Object.assign({}, options);

summary: useNewUrlParser does not seem to work
Resync SDAM tests
description: Added some mixed case hostnames into the SDAM test corpus.

summary: Fix RepliaSet tests
description:
We have two major problems with the replicaset unit tests:

1. The failover tests kill the servers while attempting to simulate a failover, which breaks almost every test that follows it. We should disable the failover tests since they do not actually run.
2. A lot of the tests following the failover tests, including "ReplSet (Operations)", " ReplSet (ReadPreference)", "SDAM", "Sessions", and 'Unicode", are broken b/c we haven't been running them to check them.
3. A lot of the "Retryable Writes" tests are passing, but are failing the session leak checker.

summary: countDocuments throws error when query matches zero docs
description:
This line:

[https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.1/lib/operations/collection_ops.js#L237]

will throw an error if the query to countDocuments does not match:
{code:java}
db.collection('somecollection').countDocuments({foo: 'blah'})
{code}
Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'n' of undefined

`docs` is an empty array if the query does not match.

summary: Update normalizedFunctionString to handle named functions
description:
The normalizedFunctionString helper needs to be updated to standardize the string representation of named functions during testing. For example, passing in either of the following functions:

function () {}

or

function func () {}

should result in the same string representation:

'function () {}'

This will fix a bug in which there are discrepancies between the results of calling toString in the browser vs. in node, which becomes relevant when testing js-bson in the browser.

summary: retryWrites=true, remove, and limit=0
TypeError: Cannot read property 'nextBatch' of undefined
description:
We are in the process of moving from a single replica set to a sharded database. After the converstion, we've started intermittently seeing this error:

{code}
TypeError: Cannot read property 'nextBatch' of undefined at Response.parse (/usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/commands.js:488:32) at /usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:564:19 at authenticateStragglers (/usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:516:16) at Connection.messageHandler (/usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/pool.js:552:5) at emitMessageHandler (/usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/connection.js:309:10) at Socket.<anonymous> (/usr/src/app/node_modules/mongodb-core/lib/connection/connection.js:452:17) at Socket.emit (events.js:182:13) at addChunk (_stream_readable.js:283:12) at readableAddChunk (_stream_readable.js:264:11) at Socket.Readable.push (_stream_readable.js:219:10)
{code}
 

Important details:
 * These errors only occur on the one collection that is actually being sharded. 
 * The initial rebalance is still under way (looks like it will take many days to complete)
 * The queries that are failing are against a 2dsphere index
 * The queries that are failing are using the following settings
 ** `raw: true`
 ** `noCursorTimeout: true`
 ** `batchSize: 500`
 * Mongo v3.6.3
 * Node driver v3.0.10
 * Node v10.4.1 (running on alpine)

 

What other information can I provide?

summary: Model.countDocuments() leads to a bug in 'mongodb/lib/operations/collection_ops.js'
Version Error
description:
Hello,

Looking on NPM version history you rollback @lastest from 3.1.1 the 3.1.0 version. But the 3.1.0 version now give me this mongo error :

"MongoError: seed list contains no mongos proxies, replicaset connections requires the parameter replicaSet to be supplied in the URI or options object, mongodb://server:port/db?replicaSet=name"

3.1.1 works good, and 3.0.9 also.

Maybe there is a problem with the rollback @latest on 3.1.0?

summary: Not master error with findOneAndUpdate
description:
If I use mongodb ReplicaSet where I connect with ReadPreference other than 'primary' and use methods which returns data and update it with same atomic operation (for example 'findAndUpdate') then sometimes I get error (depends if read goes to secondary or not):

MongoError: not master

summary: uncappedCol.isCapped() returns undefined
description: `uncapped.isCapped()` does not returns undefined. I would expect it to return a boolean false.

summary: Selecting servers by ReadPreference.nearest does not work
description: Due to a very small bug in the {{pickNearest}} methods, the list of servers is being sorted randomly instead of by {{lastIsMasterMS}} resulting in the driver not selecting the nearest servers.

summary: Link to Edit XXX on GitHub is broken
description:
----------------------------
h2. Original Description

(I don't know how this could've been overlooked for so long, so feel free to mark as a duplicate if I didn't hit the right search terms.)

On documentation pages such as [http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/tutorials/crud/,] the link to edit the page on GitHub is broken. For this page, it's [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/docs/reference/content/tutorials/crud.md]

That might explain the curiously high number of simple typos and copy/paste errors that haven't been fixed by random benevolent readers (see for example [PR #1170|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/1770]) 
----------------------------
h2. Description

h2. Scope of changes (files that need work and how much)

h2. Impact to other docs outside of this product

h2. MVP (work and date?)

h2. Resources (e.g. Scope Docs, Invision)

summary: Test bson libraries in the browser
description: Since usage of the bson libraries in the browser has increased, we should enhance our testing suites for {{js-bson}} and {{mongodb-extjson}} to run tests in the browser.

summary: Clean up / refactor / unify bulkOp code
description: There is a lot of identical code across ordered and unordered bulk ops. We should unify it and clean it up

summary: add `serialize`/`deserialize` to mongodb-extjson
description: {{mongodb-extjson}} replaced the existing {{extended-json}} module, but only provides {{stringify}} and {{parse}} methods, in sync with the {{JSON}} module. Inevitably, most users of this module implement their own methods to use some combination of {{EJSON}} and {{JSON}} to provide a way to serialize and deserialize extended json to javascript objects. We should bake these convenience methods into the module itself.

summary: Getting "port must be specified" when using connection string without ports.
description:
If I set useNewUrlParser to true and try to connect to replica set using url without ports specified, I'm getting the exception from server.js:136:24.

According to [specification|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/#standard-connection-string-format] port has to be optional.

summary: Evaluate how we handle deprecation notices
description:
Since we will be continuously deprecating out-of-date features as we drop support for server versions, we should look in a unifying approach as to how we emit deprecation notices.

Some things to consider:

* [{{util.deprecate}}|https://nodejs.org/dist/latest-v4.x/docs/api/all.html#util_util_deprecate_function_string] is available in all versions of Node that we support, and has some nifty features. It only works by wrapping a function, so we need to figure out how to incorporate this in to other deprecation notices
* Should this be integrated with options-management NODE-1256?
* Should we be logging to log files for deprecation warnings as well?

summary: "readPreference" option not honored on cursor.count()
Modernize and cleanup kerberos module
description:
(-) Add a travis badge on the {{README.md}} for the project
(-) Run {{clang-format}} on codebase
(-) Remove all compiler warnings
(-) Remove vendored base64 implementation

summary: Expose AggregationCursor
description:
It's currently not possible to do 

 ```
const { AggregationCursor, CommandCursor } = require('mongodb')
```

Whereas it works for `Cursor`.
My use case would be to simply compare `obj instanceOf AggregationCursor`. 
Of course I can do `const AggregationCursor = require('mongodb/lib/aggregation_cursor')` but that doesn't seem right. I don't see any reasons why `Cursor`  should be exposed and not `Aggregation|CommandCursor`. Also, this works out of the box with `@types/mongodb`. Here is the PR : https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/1791

Thanks

summary: Failure to clean up underlying topology when failing to initially connect
description:
We currently fail to clean up the underlying topology when a {{client.connect()}} or a {{MongoClient.connect}} fails. This causes a rapid increase in the number of connections against the server, as dead topologies still maintain their pools.

 

Additional Resources:
 * NODE-1140
 * NODE-1403
 * [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/332]
 * [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/issues/292]

summary: Update ESNext examples to use new MongoClient();
description: Basically, move away from {{MongoClient.connect}}. See https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/1793/files.

summary: Bug when parsing arrays in new url parser
Parse comma separated values in new uri parser
description: Current uri parser doesn't handle parsing comma-separated values into an array

summary: Inserts with invalid records are permitted by the driver
Inserting using `Map` assumes all keys are strings
collection.find() is called with incorrect parameters in db_ops
description: Operations that call collection.find() are using incorrect parameters, like [addUser|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/operations/db_ops.js#L94] in db_ops. We need to ensure that collection.find() is being called correctly.

summary: MongoError: cursor id 213094726591 not found
String read preference in MongoClient options not converted to ReadPreference type
Rename `getServer` to `selectServer` in old topology types
description:
This is changes does a few things for us:
- It aligns the APIs of the old and new topology types, and makes the new topology type a drop-in replacement
- It cleans up existing code that depends on this method being synchronous to use callbacks, which will be important once the underlaying topology is actually doing proper server selection

summary: `countDocuments` crash process due to wrong `handleCallback` arguments
description:
At line 236, the arguments passed to `handleCallback` is wrong, in the current published version (3.1.1) on npm: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/0ec6d30c50edfa168ed664beddceae99a9e52d0a/lib/operations/collection_ops.js#L233-L240]

Seems it has been fixed in the master branch, but this bug makes `countDocuments` crash the process whenever an error occurred and is kind of annoying.

I encountered this crash when I tried to `countDocuments(query)` with an invalid query, such as `\{ $or: [] }`.

summary: ChangeStream does not recover on resumable errors in stream mode

We performed that upgrade and continue to get the response clipped below.  

More information: Node server is in AWS EC2 Instance running the latest AMI Linux
Mongo is hosted in the same AWS region on EC2 as well.

summary: Use connection strings for all tests
description: Until now, most of our tests have been using {{newClient}} which would create a specific topology and pass that into the {{MongoClient}} constructor instead of a url. We should do what users would do, and always use connection strings to connect to our topologies.

summary: Topology `destroy` should take a callback
description: [Destroying a topology|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/blob/master/lib/topologies/server.js#L973] involves a number of I/O events, specifically when closing and destroying a pool. It's particularly useful in testing to know when this process has completed, so we should add support for passing in a callback and receiving up-to-date notification of when everything has finished closing/destroying.

summary: Typos in GSSAPI examples for NodeJS driver tutorials
description:
h2. Description
Under the URL: http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/tutorials/connect/authenticating/ section heading: "Kerberos (GSSAPI/SSPI)"

Replace: "To connect using the X.509 authentication mechanism"
With: "To connect using the GSSAPI authentication mechanism"

Replace: "MongoClient.connect(f("mongodb://%s@%s?authMechanism=GSSAPI&gssapiServiceName=mongodb", urlEncodedPrincipal, server), function(err, client) {"
With: "MongoClient.connect(f("mongodb://%s@%s/?authMechanism=GSSAPI&gssapiServiceName=mongodb", urlEncodedPrincipal, server), function(err, client) {"

h2. Scope of changes

h2. Impact to Other Docs

h2. MVP (Work and Date)

h2. Resources (Scope or Design Docs, Invision, etc.)

summary: retryWrites causing index creation to fail
Specify wholesale refactoring of options management
description:
There have been a number of refactors around how options are passed into `MongoClient`, and it is quite clear that the refactoring is not yet complete.  Recent attempts to remove deprecation warnings in the test suite have uncovered myriad cases where options are not being properly tracked (e.g. `validOptions` on the top level of MongoClient is woefully incomplete, and not even up to date with the jsdocs later in the same file). 

We need to do a deep dive into supported options from top to bottom of the driver, adding many tests along the way.

summary: APM must convert legacy messages to common format
description: From this [section|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/command-monitoring/command-monitoring.rst#implementation-notes] of the command-monitoring spec, there are a number of message types that must be "up-converted" to the common command format. As we have discovered during Dan's work getting sharded tests running again, we do not presently do this. Integration tests should be created for each, and the messages need to be converted.

summary: Implement fast fallback (happy eyeballs) for ipv4/ipv6
description: We currently explicitly attempt to connect to ipv6, falling back to ipv4 if no family is provided. We can more easily achieve our goals by properly implementing [happy eyeballs|https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Happy_Eyeballs].

summary: Handle loading of saslprep library
description:
We added an {{optionalDependency}} on {{[saslprep|https://www.npmjs.com/package/saslprep]}} to satisfy NODE-1303. {{saslprep}} is a huge library, and can add a full second to the {{mongodb}} package load-time. We originally made the decision to incur this cost at initial load: our users frequently consume our library server-side when starting up an http server, where initial load is much less important than the performance of the auth functions. However, this is causing a major impact to some testing frameworks, as seen in https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/355.

This issue is to discuss the possible solutions going forward

Option 1: Change Nothing
* Will still incur large initial load
* Will not inhibit execution of auth function

Option 2: Load {{saslprep}} upon first execution of SCRAM-SHA-256 auth
* Avoids incurring cost if SCRAM-SHA-256 is not used
* Adds enormous cost to first execution of SCRAM-SHA-256 auth

Option 3: Switch {{saslprep}} to {{peerOptionalDependency}}
* Will not inhibit execution of auth function
* Users concerned with performance can choose not to install {{saslprep}}, avoiding high load cost
* Users will not get {{saslprep}} for free anymore, will have to explicitly opt in. *This will likely be a breaking change*

summary: Removal of APM metadata broke instrumentations.
description:
This is a continuation of [this conversation|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/commit/b7ae8cd70c90d3c14d22708869335b31c39b148f#commitcomment-29690823].

 

The {{instrument}} API does _not_ work as before. Previously the 2nd argument to the callback was an array of endpoints describing how to instrument those methods. Now it is an instance of the {{Instrumentation}} class which has no information about the shape of the classes in the library.

If this method of instrumenting Mongo is truly deprecated, the [documentation which still lists the old way of instrumenting|http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/reference/management/apm/] should be updated.

 

That all said, there is a serious limitation of the command monitoring API when it comes to Node instrumentation, and that's a lack of mechanism for proper context propagation. Since only begin and end events are emitted, there is no way for us to properly restore and remove context without leaking it. This is uniquely a problem for Node due to its evented nature, which is probably why only the Node driver implemented the metadata array.

summary: Add default, required, and deprecated to options schema validator
description: Once the options schema validator validates option types, we need to add support for _default, required,_ and _deprecated_.

summary: Refactor Auth to exclusively use a MongoCredential object
description:
According to the [auth spec|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/auth/auth.rst#mongocredential], we *should* be using an object called {{MongoCredentials}} to pass around all of our auth configuration. Right now, we have a variety of ways we pass around credentials, including:

* Sticking things together in an anonymous {{auth}} object
* Sticking things in an {{auth}} array
* Just passing fields around individually

By switching to tracking credentials in a single way, it would simplify a lot of code.

summary: Remove duplicated code from auth providers
description: There is currently a lot of auth code that is just replicated across auth providers, and makes a lot of noise. We should reconcile it all into one base class

summary: Deprecate eval helpers
description: See DRIVERS-460 for details.

summary: Kerberos Service Name Never Anything Other Than "mongodb"
description:
When connecting with a URI and providing a "gssapiServiceName" in the URI, the value never gets used. This is because after the url_parser sets the property `gssapiServiceName` in the options, it gets converted to all lower case afterwards and thus mongodb-core always gets undefined when grabbing the property.

This is the line that the property access always returns undefined and thus results in the service name being set to "mongodb": [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/blob/b553ee17e7377b4943af29ce0847598a246ed6f2/lib/auth/gssapi.js#L49]

 

At this line the property has been already converted to lowercase: [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/authenticate.js#L66] (see my screen shot for the options object at that point)

 

In the url_parser the value is not all lower case: https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/url_parser.js#L464

summary: Integrate single `Topology` from Core into Native
description: The porcelain layer should use the newly created single topology type from {{mongodb-core}}, and reduce its variants to a single type as well.

summary: Reintroduce custom property support to new uri parser
description: The old uri parser has a notion of validating known properties, for example [authMechanismProperties|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/master/lib/url_parser.js#L498-L518]. This needs to be ported to the new URI parser

summary: Replicaset tests for core use Server and require primary on port 31000
description: Many of the mongodb-core tests for replicasets use a {{Server}} instance and directly connect to what is generally the primary (port 31000). If this ever changes then things break dramatically. We should convert all of these tests to use {{configuration.newTopology}}.

summary: New URL parser rejects db names made only of numbers
description:
Originally reported here: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/COMPASS-3090

 

Was able to recreate by connecting to the node driver directly and trying to authenticate with a db name of "001". Authentication will fail if \{useNewUrlParser: true} is passed, otherwise no error.

summary: Fix auth workflow with regards to auth mechanism resolution
description:
See [this comment | https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/348#discussion_r217726742].

* If you do not provide a mechanism, or if you say "default" we need do to mechanism negotiation. As of MongoDB 4.0, this requires an ismaster to get saslSupportedMechs
* For mechanism negotiation to work, we need to have credentials available at the time of topology connect, which happens before the actual auth call. This way, we can add the saslSupportedMechs field to the first outgoing ismaster
* At the same time, there is legacy support for authenticating on connect, where you pass credentials in to the initial connect call, and immediately auth. This is a problem, since the credentials have not been mechanism negotiated yet, so they will still say default.

Lets find a way to do this that is neater

summary: SCRAM-SHA-256 tests are not actually run against replicaset
description: Our current {{SCRAM-SHA-256}} tests are not actually run against a replica set even when the environment is specified, because the URL omits a {{replicaSet}} option. Instead, the tests are run in single mode against one of the nodes.

summary: Add dynamicDefaults/overrides to options validator
description: Some options, like {{readPreference}}, have default values that are more complicated than others, like {{capped}}. We should add a dynamicDefaults/overrides object as an argument to the options validator. This will allow us to properly set defaults with complex logic or inheritance hierarchies.

summary: GridFSBucket upload stream throw deprecation warning
description: DeprecationWarning: collection.insert is deprecated. Use insertOne, insertMany or bulkWrite instead.

Implement assertArity function
description: We need an {{assertArity}} function for options validation without the OperationBuilder. This function will check that operations are called with the correct number of arguments.

summary: Implement auth connection string tests
Port authentication support to new Topology class
description: The new unified {{Topology}} class lacks support for authentication, it should be ported from the existing topologies.

summary: Update atlas connectivity tests to use new auth credentials
description: Aut has been fully refactored for both native and core drivers, the old Atlas connectivity tests will no longer work as written and need to be updated.

summary: Setting useNewUrlParser: true in connection causes issue.
Driver overwrites database from uri with authSource from options
description:
Configuration:
* user execute query in "DB_query"
* user authorization stored in "DB_auth" (user has no acces to that db)
* config:
  url: mongodb://user:password@host:port/DB_query
  options: {"authSource": "DB_auth"}

const client = await MongoClient.connect(url, options);
const result = await client.db().collection(collection).find(query, fields);

Expected: query executed in "DB_query"
Actual: query executed in "DB_auth"

This thing seems caused by [this change|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/commit/923ceb0176341370fdda573ffb6d11024d1dc81e] i get error "MongoError: not authorized on DB_auth to execute command".
Db in url is differ than than authSource, after latest upgrade driver try to execute query in authSource instead of db specified in url.

Behavior exists in 3.x and 4.x mongodb, standalone and replica set

summary: unify collation support in topology
docs tutorial shows connecting with MongoClient() incorrectly
Test deleteMany and updateMany with retryWrites=true
description: See DRIVERS-574 for details.

summary: Document properties of KerberosServer and KerberosClient
description: The newly introduced {{KerberosServer}} and {{KerberosClient}} classes in the kerberos module have documentation for attached methods, but not the properties associated with them. These should be documented.

summary: node driver api createIndex() can't capture specific error
kerberos module makes incorrect null checks for user information
Kerberos Authentication on Windows should not require password
description:
Hi,

*Current behavior*
The driver requires the username (user principal name) and password when using the GSSAPI authentication on Windows. 

*Expected behavior*
Only the UPN should be required for Kerberos auth on Windows.

Thanks,

Davenson Lombard

summary: Code of ensureIndex and createIndex are inconsistent, yet the docs state they should be an alias of one another
description:
The [documentation|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/method/db.collection.ensureIndex/] of MongoDB states that `ensureIndex` should be deprecated, and an alias of `createIndex`:

{quote}db.collection.ensureIndex(keys, options)
Deprecated since version 3.0.0: db.collection.ensureIndex() is now an alias for db.collection.createIndex().
{quote}

However, upon analysing the code, they seem to have clearly different functionality and `ensureIndex` does not in fact look like a simple alias for `createIndex`:

*createIndex*
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L1029

*ensureIndex*
https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/3.0.0/lib/db.js#L1113

Expected behaviour is that in light of the deprecation, `ensureIndex` should simply call `createIndex`.

Currently there seem to be some users who still rely on the side effects present in `ensureIndex`, but they should probably receive a deprecation notice as well as instructions on how to migrate to using `createIndex` instead.

summary: Fix sporadic build failures in mongodb-core
Remove circular references to types in js-bson
description: Each type defined in {{js-bson}} has a [circular reference to itself|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/master/lib/bson/code.js#L25-L26]. We should remove these in the next major version of the module.

summary: Fully validate options for all operations
description: There are likely operations for which all options are not currently documented throughout the driver. We need to fully trace options for every operation and ensure all schemas are correct.

summary: js-bson should allow 0xFFFD to be encoded/decoded
description:
https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/277

See: https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/blob/v3.0.2/lib/bson/parser/deserializer.js#L130. When we deserialize BSON, we take any occurrence of 0xFFFD to be a serializer error, which does not account for the fact that a user may want to intentionally encode 0xFFFD as a character.

Options to resolve them:

1. Use https://www.npmjs.com/package/utf8
Pros: Will throw on failure to decode utf8
Cons: Seems to not use buffers?

2. Use https://www.npmjs.com/package/utf-8-validate
Pros: Same as above
Cons: Is C++

3. Buffer.from(input.toString()).compare(input) === 0
Pros: Barely need to do anything
Cons: Needs alloc of new Buffer. Could be bad for large strings
Cons: Can we use Buffer.compare at specific index positions?
Cons: Will this work in the browser?

4. Write our own utf8 validator
Pros: It will be tailored to our needs
Cons: Time, possibility for errors

summary: CI Test against Node 10.x (Dubnium)
description:
Node 10.x (dubnium) entered Active LTS on October 31st, 2018. We should start testing against it in all of our CI environments.

 
 * -Travis node-mongodb-native- Already Done
 * -Evergreen node-mongodb-native- [https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/pull/1860]
 * -Travis mongodb-core-: [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]
 * -Evergreen mongodb-core-: [https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]
 * -Travis js-bson- Already done
 * Travis bson-ext <-- Not sure if this is necessary.
 * -Travis mongodb-extjson- Unnecessary; library getting merged into js-bson

summary: BSON ObjectID constructor should throw if passed-in string uses characters greater than 8 bits.
description:
Ported from [https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/281]

 
{quote}There have been some similar issues open for this before ([#106|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/106], [#112|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/112]), but I don't think they made the right point.
 The issue happens indeed because the argument is not validated.

Providing {{new ObjectId('000000000000')}} gives the correct console.log of: {{303030303030303030303030}}.

Javascript strings are UTF-16 encoded. I create my ObjectId using:
 {{new ObjectId('ŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽŽ')}}
 where Ž is this latin letter [http://www.ltg.ed.ac.uk/~richard/utf-8.cgi?input=%C5%BD&mode=char]

The construction doesn't fail, but while trying to console.log this ObjectId gives:
 {{undefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefinedundefined}}
{quote}
To fix this, we should throw if a length-12 string is passed in with characters with a charCode > 256

summary: Don't issue warning for missing kerberos module
description: The core driver attempts to optionally require kerberos and spits out a warning if it can't find it, see [here|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/blob/master/lib/utils.js#L48]. This is annoying for users who aren't using kerberos, we should remove it.

summary: Explain useNewUrlParser
description: At [http://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/3.1/api/MongoClient.html], please explain what *useNewUrlParser* does. There's [a lot of confusion on StackOverflow|https://stackoverflow.com/questions/50448272/avoid-current-url-string-parser-is-deprecated-warning-by-setting-usenewurlpars] about that.

summary: Integrate options validation into Admin, Collection, Cursor, Db, MongoClient
description: Once options validation and the OperationBuilder are implemented, we can integrate them into all operations.

summary: Extended JSON should default to using native number type in non-strict mode
description:
From [~fonger]'s pull request

bq. MongoDB node.js driver will store JavaScript Number as Double type by default. So I think parsing in non-strict mode, Double should cast to number. Furthermore, JavaScript numbers are always stored as double precision floating point numbers, following the IEEE 754 standard.

see [this pull request|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-extjson/pull/21]

summary: Modernize and improve bundling of js-bson
description:
* Update all of js-bson to use ES6 notation where it makes sense
* Standardize what is exported from the module
* Integrate bundle changes from [~eric.daniels]
* Publish v4.0.0 of the module

summary: Update Travis version of MongoDB
description:
From [this PR|https://github.com/mongodb-js/mongodb-core/pull/371]:

bq. we should bump the default tested version of mongo from 3.4 to something more reasonable

summary: Validate options in findAndModify and findAndRemove
description: {{findAndModify}} and {{findAndRemove}} are deprecated, but still used internally. Because of their internal use, we should add their options to options validation for any method that calls them.

summary: New url parser ignores auth provide via options
description: In my haste to pass auth spec tests, I ignored the fact that we support passing in auth information through options in {{MongoClient}}s constructor.

summary: Authentication fails when database is not specified in connection string
Need to double-URI-encode username/password with mongodb+srv:// protocol
Mongos topology leaks memory on server reconnect
Db should take in client instead of topology
description: If {{Db}} takes in {{client}} instead of {{topology}}, we can use the {{client}}'s {{optionsValidationLevel}} and replace every {{topology}} with {{client.topology}}.

summary: kerberos module does not honor optional nature of `defaultDomain` in `checkPassword`
description: See the following [github issue|https://github.com/mongodb-js/kerberos/issues/91] for more details.

summary: Latest BSON library unable to serialize ObjectID from previous versions
description: See [github issue|https://github.com/mongodb/js-bson/issues/289]

summary: Evaluate and correct monitoring in new SDAM layer
description: The current rewrite of SDAM failed to

summary: GridFS Options Management
description: Add options validation for \{{GridFS}} operations.

summary: Simplify cursor `nextFunction` to combine cursor initialization and initial find command
description: The current implementation of core's {{nextFunction}} is incredibly complex, and makes a number of recursive calls needlessly. We should simplify this code path, primarily ensuring that cursor initialization also makes the first find command call. This will allows for the next step in the wire protocol refactor, which is to make {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} actually take a callback, and provide a consistent API across wire protocol methods.

summary: Replace "shallowClone" util function with Object.assign calls
description: {{shallowClone(x)}} can be accomplished with native {{Object.assign({}, x)}}, and that is an understood idiom. We should get rid of the unnecessary util function in our code.

summary: Introduce wire protocol method `query`
description: The {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} has a hack to introspect the incoming command, determine if it is a find command, and switch its internal logic if so. We shouldn't have to introspect here, we can simply call a different command when we are attempting a query. Additionally, this will allow us to defer checking whether commands are supported when executing a query, switching between a plain legacy {{OP_QUERY}} and a modern, "commandified" {{OP_QUERY}} against the {{$cmd}} collection.

summary: Modernize and clean up WireProtocol implementation
description: Convert the WireProtocol to a class, remove duplication and streamline options passing

summary: Refactor wire protocol's command method to take a callback
description:
The {{WireProtocol.prototype.command}} method is the current odd duck out in the methods provided on the wire protocol handler, in that it actually just _prepares_ a command, rather than executing it. This is done primarily for cursors, since they build the command and save it off internally. Refactoring this method to take a command accomplishes a few things:
 * Makes the API for this class consistent, allowing for easy refactoring of the actual code - or even allowing the code to be moves to another location (perhaps directly on {{Connection}}, so we can move server selection elsewhere
 * Allows locations in the code that are building their own {{Query}} instances to reuse the {{command}} method, so that we can reduce the number of places we have to make wire protocol based decisions (is collation supported?) 
 * Once all locations in the code are using this path, we can easily switch between OP_QUERY and OP_MSG, satisfying the full implementation of OP_MSG

summary: New Topology layer fails to clear monitor on close
description: When the {{Topology}} object is closed, it should also clear any existing monitoring processes, which it currently does not. This keeps resources open, blocks the event loop, and generally leaks memory.

summary: ReplSet and Mongos topologies do not apply connect options
description: The legacy ReplSet and Mongos classes do not apply options passed into connect. This was identified by the fact that compression information was not propagating to child {{Server}} instances.

summary: Use hasOwnProperty to check for options that need to be type-checked
description: The options validator currently [checks for options|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/next/lib/options_validator.js#L146] to validate without using {{hasOwnProperty}}, which is incorrect.

summary: Document support for `readConcern` on operation level
description: We support {{readConcern}} on an operation level, but actually only apply it to {{find}} commands. We should firstly document all places where we support {{readConcern}} as a passed option, and secondly support it on all read operations, providing tests proving that it is indeed supported in all those cases.

summary: Separate options validation from application of default options
description: The options validator currently validates user-provided options and applies default options, including dynamic defaults like {{readPreference}}. We should separate this logic so every operation makes a call to {{validate}} and then a call to {{applyDefaults}}. This enables us to skip {{validate}} and only use {{applyDefaults}} at the bottom level of operations ({{collection_ops.js}}, {{db_ops.js}}, {{mongo_client_ops.js}}, etc.).

summary: BulkWrite: Incorrect batch-size calculations causes batch to fail
description:
related to: https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/NODE-1298

driver's calculation result is 16777058

mongodb's calculation result is 16908004 which is greater than 16777058.

It can be reproduced with mongodb3.6.

summary: Refactor all wire protocol methods to reuse command where possible
description: All wire protocol methods in 3.2 support, and most method in 2.6 support, are actually issuing "commands" ({{OP_QUERY}} messages directed to a {{*.$cmd}} namespace). Each of these methods should internally use the {{command}} method, in order to deduplicate the options management of these messages, as well as to localize checks for e.g. collation support.

summary: Collection.find applying collation to command without checks
description:
It looks like the collection.find method is [looping over user-given options|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/297d843fa016f7ad11545a620916ab5562bf2fd2/lib/collection.js#L386-L390] (including collation, if given) and applying them to the command without further checking. This means that collation will be set on the command before it even [gets to the decorateWithCollation call|https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native/blob/297d843fa016f7ad11545a620916ab5562bf2fd2/lib/collection.js#L427], which furthermore implies that collation can be incorrectly passed through to the final command / decorateWithCollation is essentially not doing anything.

I noticed this when I commented out the decorateWithCollation call and no tests broke.
